note_text: 65 year old male presents with sudden onset severe chest pain radiating to the left arm and jaw, associated with diaphoresis and shortness of breath. Patient is pale and diaphoretic. History of hypertension and diabetes. ECG shows ST elevation in leads II, III, aVF. Troponin pending.

72 year old female brought in by ambulance after collapse at home. Patient found unresponsive by family. No pulse detected on arrival. Cardiac arrest initiated. CPR in progress. No prior cardiac history documented.

45 year old male with known bee allergy presents minutes after sting with anaphylaxis. Severe throat swelling, difficulty breathing, urticaria covering torso. Patient extremely distressed, hypotension developing rapidly.

58 year old male admitted from ICU with worsening respiratory failure. Patient on high-flow oxygen, saturation not improving. Progressive dyspnea over past 6 hours. Bilateral crackles on auscultation. Suspected acute respiratory distress syndrome.

34 year old female with septic shock following urosepsis. Hypotension not responsive to initial fluid resuscitation. Altered mental status, confusion and agitation. Rigors and fever. Lactate markedly elevated. Sepsis protocol activated.

55 year old male with severe chest pain onset 2 hours ago. Pain described as crushing, 8/10 intensity, radiating to left shoulder. Associated with shortness of breath and nausea. Diaphoretic on exam. History of hyperlipidemia and smoking.

68 year old female with sudden onset severe dyspnea. Pleuritic chest pain on right side with hemoptysis. History of recent long-haul flight. Lower limb swelling noted. Pulmonary embolism suspected pending CT-PA.

80 year old male with altered mental status and confusion. Family reports acute change over past 4 hours. Patient disoriented to time and place. History of dementia but this is an acute change. Fever present. Blood cultures ordered.

29 year old female with known asthma presenting with acute severe dyspnea. Unable to complete sentences. Accessory muscle use noted. Peak flow significantly reduced. Not responding to initial nebulisation. Moderate tachypnea. Wheezing bilateral.

61 year old male with suspected stroke. Sudden onset slurred speech and right-sided weakness. Facial droop noted on left. FAST positive. History of atrial fibrillation, not anticoagulated. Symptom onset 90 minutes ago.

50 year old male with worsening shortness of breath and bilateral leg swelling over 3 days. Orthopnea requiring 3 pillows. History of heart failure and COPD. Weight gain of 4kg in one week. Tachycardia on arrival.

42 year old female presenting with moderate chest pain and shortness of breath after exertion. Symptoms resolved at rest. No diaphoresis. No radiation. History of hypertension, currently on amlodipine. ECG unremarkable.

33 year old male with fever of 3 days duration, productive cough, and mild shortness of breath. Reduced air entry at right base. CXR showing right lower lobe consolidation consistent with pneumonia. No sepsis criteria met.

75 year old female with palpitations and mild dizziness for 2 days. No syncope. No chest pain. History of paroxysmal atrial fibrillation. Rate controlled. Currently in AF on ECG with ventricular rate 108.

52 year old male with diabetic ketoacidosis. Blood glucose 24 mmol/L with moderate ketonuria. Complains of nausea and vomiting and abdominal pain. Clinically dehydrated. Not in shock. No altered consciousness.

67 year old female presenting with moderate shortness of breath and peripheral edema. Mild tachycardia. History of chronic heart failure. Taking furosemide at home. CXR shows cardiomegaly and pulmonary vascular redistribution.

28 year old female with mild headache and fatigue for 2 days. Denies chest pain. No shortness of breath. No fever. Denies nausea or vomiting. Blood pressure slightly elevated at baseline but has been managing with lifestyle changes.

19 year old male presents for routine follow-up after mild ankle sprain 1 week ago. No significant complaints. Denies syncope, palpitations, or dizziness. Ambulating well on crutches. No swelling at ankle. No shortness of breath.

44 year old male presenting with mild nausea and loose stools for 24 hours. No blood in stool. No vomiting. No fever. Tolerating oral fluids well. Denies abdominal pain. No significant medical history.

35 year old female with mild cough and sore throat for 3 days. No shortness of breath. No chest pain. No fever. No history of asthma. Chest clear on auscultation. Likely viral upper respiratory tract infection.
